concerns.text: FR-009 and US-2 require OLS refitting for ALL three selection methods (Forward Stepwise, Backward Elimination, LASSO) to calculate p-values. Task T027 explicitly limits scope to 'LASSO-selected variables', leaving FR-009 unimplemented for the other two methods.

US-1 Acceptance Scenario 2 and Spec FR-002 require simulation for Sparsity levels {0.1, 0.2, 0.4}. Task T016 configures levels as 'including 0.2 and 0.4, and other representative values', which is ambiguous and risks omitting the required 0.1 level.

FR-005 and US-3 explicitly require Kruskal-Wallis tests on 'simulation-level mean power' (aggregated per condition). Task T035 describes performing the test on 'simulation-level mean power' but the preceding task T034b creates an *additional* aggregation step, creating ambiguity on whether the test runs on raw data (n=24,000) or aggregated means (n=conditions), risking violation of the unit of analysis.

FR-002 requires a 'sufficient number' of simulations determined by a pilot run (per Plan). Task T012 hardcodes an assertion for exactly 24,000 rows, contradicting the spec's requirement for dynamic sufficiency determination and risking failure if the pilot dictates a different count.

Constitution Principle VI (Simulation Fidelity) and VII (Selection Method Transparency) require logging seed, SNR, and Sparsity for *every* run. Task T026 only specifies logging 'selected variables and thresholds', omitting the mandatory simulation parameters (seed, SNR, Sparsity) in the log entry.
Plan.md Project Structure explicitly defines `data/processed/` for simulation results. Task T001 creates `data/simulated/`, creating a path inconsistency with the plan's defined structure.
